data.title: Equus Energy Advances Equus Gas Project as WA Gas Shortfall Looms
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Equus Energy Limited ( (AU:EQU) ) has shared an update.

Equus Energy reported that pre-front end engineering and design work on its Equus Gas Project is nearing completion, with technical studies and development planning maturing two competing tie-back concepts to existing LNG and domestic gas facilities. The project’s proximity to established North West Shelf infrastructure is intended to lower upfront capital costs, accelerate market access and support finalisation of a field development plan, cost estimates and project economics.

Alongside the technical work, Equus advanced preparations for a structured partnering process to facilitate future project development, potential sell-downs and offtake agreements, underpinned by ongoing study cost contributions from Alcoa under a funding and gas sales agreement. The company framed these efforts against a tightening domestic gas outlook in Western Australia, where forecast supply shortfalls from around 2030 enhance the strategic relevance of new gas developments that can leverage existing infrastructure.

More about Equus Energy Limited

Equus Energy Limited is an Australian oil and gas company focused on developing its 100%-owned Equus Gas Project offshore Western Australia. The company is targeting capital-efficient gas production via tie-backs to existing offshore platforms, pipelines and onshore LNG and domestic gas infrastructure to supply both export and local markets.

Average Trading Volume: 387,387

Current Market Cap: A$20.6M

data.title: Aker Solutions Books Strong Orders and Cash Boost as 2026 Revenue Normalizes
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Aker Solutions ASA ( (GB:0QXP) ) has shared an announcement.

Aker Solutions, a global energy engineering and services company, reported that revenues are normalizing after a peak year, but first-quarter 2026 results remained strong with revenue of NOK 13.4 billion, EBITDA of NOK 1.2 billion and an 8.6 percent EBITDA margin. The group’s order intake reached NOK 28.8 billion, driven mainly by long-term maintenance and modification frame agreements in Norway with Equinor and Aker BP, lifting its secured order backlog to NOK 80.2 billion and underscoring solid demand for its services.

The company’s financial position strengthened further, with net cash rising to NOK 8.7 billion and the sale of its SLB shares for NOK 2.5 billion providing additional flexibility. Aker Solutions expects 2026 revenue of around NOK 50 billion and an underlying EBITDA margin of 7.0 to 7.5 percent, and has underscored its shareholder-friendly capital allocation with a combined ordinary and extraordinary dividend of NOK 8.60 per share, while also investing in emerging opportunities such as small modular reactors to support long-term growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:0QXP) stock is a Sell with a NOK35.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Aker Solutions ASA stock, see the GB:0QXP Stock Forecast page.

More about Aker Solutions ASA

Aker Solutions is a global provider of integrated solutions, products and services to the energy industry, with a focus on enabling low-carbon oil and gas production and developing renewable energy solutions. Leveraging digital technologies and large-scale project execution, the company supports the transition to more sustainable energy systems and employs about 11,700 people across 13 countries.

data.title: Compagnie Financière Tradition Delivers Double-Digit Q1 2026 Growth on Strong Interdealer Broking
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Compagnie Financiere Tradition SA ( (CH:CFT) ) has provided an update.

Compagnie Financière Tradition SA, a major global interdealer broker in financial and commodity-related products, operates in more than 30 countries and serves institutional clients with broking and data services across a wide spectrum of financial instruments and energy and environmental markets. The company employs over 2,500 people worldwide and is listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange, positioning it as a key player in wholesale financial markets.

The group reported strong first-quarter 2026 performance, with consolidated revenue including its share of joint ventures rising 17.4% at constant exchange rates to CHF 339.7 million, or 5.5% at current rates. Growth was driven primarily by the interdealer broking business, which advanced 17.6% at constant exchange rates, while management highlighted that a notable currency base effect, particularly involving the U.S. dollar against the Swiss franc, boosted year-on-year growth in early 2026 but is expected to fade as the year progresses if exchange rates stabilise.

Reported IFRS revenue increased 18.5% at constant exchange rates and 6.6% at current rates to CHF 314.2 million, underscoring resilient underlying activity despite currency headwinds. The retail-focused non-interdealer segment saw a small decline in reported revenue but still posted double-digit growth at constant exchange rates, suggesting that the group’s core wholesale franchise remains the chief engine of expansion while overall results remain sensitive to foreign exchange movements that could temper reported growth later in the year.

More about Compagnie Financiere Tradition SA

Compagnie Financière Tradition SA is one of the world’s largest interdealer brokers in financial and commodity-related products, operating in over 30 countries and employing more than 2,500 people. The group provides broking and data services across money market instruments, bonds, interest rate, currency and credit derivatives, equities and equity derivatives, futures, and non-financial products such as energy, environmental products, and precious metals, and its shares are listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange.

Average Trading Volume: 2,974

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: CHF2.28B

data.title: Nankai Reshapes Top Management After Railway Spin-Off, Appoints Okajima as CEO
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Nankai Electric Railway Co., Ltd. ( (JP:9044) ) is now available.

Nankai Co., Ltd. has announced a leadership change in conjunction with its new group management structure following the spin-off of its railway business in April 2026. The Board has resolved to appoint Representative Director and President Okajima Nobuyuki as CEO, effective June 16, 2026, while Representative Director and Chairman Achikita Teruhiko will step down from the CEO role to concentrate on external engagement.

The company aims to accelerate decision-making, strengthen competitiveness, and expand its real estate and new business pillars under this revised governance framework. By vesting internal business execution authority in Okajima and positioning Achikita to focus on collaboration with local governments and cross-industry partners, Nankai is seeking to enhance group corporate value and reinforce its management accountability structure for the next stage of growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:9044) stock is a Buy with a Yen2900.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Nankai Electric Railway Co., Ltd. stock, see the JP:9044 Stock Forecast page.

More about Nankai Electric Railway Co., Ltd.

Nankai Co., Ltd., listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market, is a core company of the Nankai Group, historically centered on railway operations and increasingly focused on expanding its real estate business. The group also seeks to develop new businesses that will stand alongside its railway and real estate segments, while deepening collaboration with municipalities along its railway lines and partners in other industries.

data.title: Nippon Coke & Engineering Tightens Net Asset Covenants on Syndicated Loans
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Nippon Coke & Engineering Company, Limited ( (JP:3315) ) has provided an update.

Nippon Coke & Engineering has amended the financial covenants on multiple syndicated loan agreements used to fund capital expenditures and working capital, aligning them with recent changes to its commitment line. The revised terms reset the net asset maintenance benchmark to 80% of net assets as of the fiscal year ended March 2026, replacing the previous standard that referenced earlier fiscal years while retaining the condition that operating income must not be negative for two consecutive years.

The adjustments apply to a 2022 committed term loan and three tranches of a 2024 syndicated facility, together totaling nearly ¥38.7 billion in commitments, and are expected to influence the firm’s longer-term balance sheet management and capital policy. The company said the financial impact of these covenant changes will be reflected in its consolidated forecast for the year ending March 2027, to be disclosed alongside its March 2026 results in mid-May, signaling that lenders and investors should watch for updated guidance on earnings and leverage metrics.

More about Nippon Coke & Engineering Company, Limited

Nippon Coke & Engineering Co., Ltd., listed on the TSE Prime Market under securities code 3315, operates in the coke and engineering sector and relies on sizable syndicated loans to fund capital expenditures and working capital. The company borrows on an unsecured basis from consortia of major city and regional banks, reflecting a longstanding dependence on bank financing for its long-term investment needs.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: NZ Super Fund Adjusts Substantial Stake in KMD Brands After Equity Raise
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from KMD Brands Limited ( (NZ:KMD) ).

New Zealand Superannuation Fund Nominees Limited, as nominee for the state-owned New Zealand Superannuation Fund, has disclosed an updated substantial holding in KMD Brands, now owning 201,502,056 ordinary shares, or 11.198% of the company. The change reflects both on-market sales and the allotment of new shares under KMD’s recent retail entitlement offer, slightly reducing NZSF’s percentage stake but maintaining it as a major long-term institutional shareholder, a signal of continued sovereign fund backing that remains relevant for other investors monitoring KMD’s register.

Between 14 and 28 April 2026, the fund sold 3,554,638 KMD shares on market while simultaneously participating in the retail shortfall bookbuild, acquiring 5,049,686 new shares as part of KMD’s capital-raising programme. The resulting mix of secondary sales and primary issuance has modestly diluted NZSF’s proportionate holding due to an enlarged share base, underscoring the impact of KMD’s equity raising on shareholder positions and the company’s evolving ownership structure.

The most recent analyst rating on (NZ:KMD) stock is a Buy with a N$0.14 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on KMD Brands Limited stock, see the NZ:KMD Stock Forecast page.

More about KMD Brands Limited

KMD Brands Limited is a outdoor apparel and equipment company best known for its Kathmandu, Rip Curl and Oboz brands, targeting adventure and lifestyle consumers in New Zealand, Australia and global markets. The group focuses on performance clothing, surfwear and footwear, serving both retail and wholesale channels and positioning itself as a specialist in outdoor and travel gear.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: N$102.9M

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Invictus Energy Limited ( (AU:IVZ) ) has provided an announcement.

Invictus Energy has issued 4,166,667 new shares as payment for services in lieu of fees and a further 136 shares following the conversion of listed options, expanding its capital base without raising cash. The company confirmed the shares were issued without a prospectus under Australian Corporations Act provisions and stated it remains compliant with continuous disclosure and financial reporting obligations, with no undisclosed price-sensitive information identified for investors.

This technical cleansing notice assures the market that Invictus’ new securities can trade freely while affirming transparency and regulatory compliance around its capital management. The move underscores the company’s reliance on equity-based consideration to support its ongoing high-impact exploration activities in Zimbabwe’s Cabora Bassa Basin.

More about Invictus Energy Limited

Invictus Energy Ltd is an independent upstream oil and gas exploration company focused on high-impact energy resources in sub-Saharan Africa, particularly within Zimbabwe’s Cabora Bassa Basin. The Perth-headquartered, ASX-listed company has made a significant gas discovery at the Mukuyu field and continues to mature this frontier onshore African basin through ongoing exploration activities.

data.title: Critical Resources widens solid-state battery push with NTU thermal management licence
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Critical Resources Ltd ( (AU:CRR) ).

Critical Resources has secured an evaluation licence from NTUitive, the commercial arm of Nanyang Technological University in Singapore, for a portfolio of thermal management technologies covering lithium-ion batteries and high‑density electronics in data centres. The deal extends the company’s solid-state battery evaluation programme, linking its upstream lithium supply from Mavis Lake with downstream battery development and advanced cooling solutions.

The NTU technology uses a two-phase spray cooling system with a dielectric fluid that evaporates on contact with hot components, delivering significantly higher cooling efficiency and lower energy use than conventional air‑cooled systems. Validated research results show sharp cuts in facility energy consumption and chip temperatures, and the inclusion of model predictive control offers a potential pathway to smarter battery and electronics thermal management in hot climates, improving Critical Resources’ positioning in both energy storage and data-centre infrastructure markets.

More about Critical Resources Ltd

Critical Resources Limited is an ASX-listed company focused on the lithium value chain, leveraging its Mavis Lake Lithium Project to support downstream development of next-generation solid-state lithium-ion batteries. The company is expanding beyond raw material supply into battery technology innovation, targeting applications that demand reliable performance in elevated-temperature environments.

Average Trading Volume: 4,445,556
